message:
fix: apply backpressure to bash output pump threads to prevent OOM

The bash command output pump threads used unbounded channels, which could
cause unbounded memory growth if a child process produced output faster
than the async loop could drain and spill to disk. Switch both run_bash_rpc
and run_bash_command to bounded sync_channel(1024) so pump threads block
on send() when the buffer fills, which propagates backpressure through
the OS pipe buffer to pause the child process.

Also fixes spill_failed flag not being set after detecting the hard size
limit in both ingest_bash_rpc_chunk and ingest_bash_chunk, which caused
repeated warning log spam.

Simplifies SSE UTF-8 error handling by removing unused first_error
tracking — invalid bytes are already skipped gracefully and the error
was never actionable by callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
